spec_match.rationale: Uses position-area (the new name) on a popover anchored to a target, demonstrates the property values exactly as the renamed spec allows.

interactive.rationale: Tab bar with six direction values drives a live re-render of the popover position. CSS.supports probe reports parser acceptance.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'switch the side and see live behaviour' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Demo only uses position-area; never sets inset-area to show the deprecated property still works during the migration window. Missing the spec's headline behaviour: that both names currently resolve identically.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout; --accent-blue popover with --bg-ivory text passes AA.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the rename, not just the new name
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of this feature is the RENAME from inset-area to position-area. Currently the demo only shows position-area in isolation. Add a side-by-side panel or toggle so visitors can see inset-area still working (during the migration window) and position-area producing identical output.

openQuestions.title: Add a deprecation warning indicator
openQuestions.detail: Visitors moving an existing codebase want to know when DevTools warns. Capture console.warn output (or annotate the source) so the deprecation message Chrome 131 emits is visible inline.

summary: Functional position-area demo with live direction toggling — but never actually demonstrates the rename, only the new name in isolation. Missing the headline behaviour: that inset-area still works (during the migration window) and emits a deprecation warning.
